**Total count: 11 unresolved issues**
I'll help you find all unresolved Sentry issues in the 'billing' project. Let me first explore the structure of the sentry_issues table to understand what columns are available.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'sentry_issues' ORDER BY ordinal_position"}

text: Now I can query for unresolved Sentry issues in the 'billing' project. I'll look for issues where the status is not 'resolved' and the project is 'billing'.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT id, title, status, project, count, first_seen, last_seen 
FROM sentry_issues 
WHERE project = 'billing' AND status != 'resolved' 
ORDER BY last_seen DESC"}

data: {"rows":[{"id":"SENTRY-3015","title":"OOMKilled: SSO SCIM provisioning errors","status":"unresolved","project":"billing","count":524,"first_seen":"2026-05-16T00:00:00.000Z","last_seen":"2026-05-31T00:00:00.000Z"},{"id":"SENTRY-3002","title":"5xx: CSV export truncates large accounts","status":"unreso…(+2160)

text: Perfect! Now let me get just the IDs and count how many there are:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id FROM sentry_issues WHERE project = 'billing' AND status != 'resolved' ORDER BY id"}

text: Let me also get the exact count

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) as unresolved_count FROM sentry_issues WHERE project = 'billing' AND status != 'resolved'"}
